commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=51124e890868f7f1e0434f80d6405ea4a3a47885)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a `tts.status` command, TTS provider readiness/fallback resolution, fallback-aware speak results, setup/MCP/skill docs, and shared/setup regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows `ResolveEffectiveProvider("piper", ..., readyProviders: {"windows"})` returns effective `windows` with `FellBack=true`, so an explicit provider override no longer reaches the existing Piper not-downloaded error path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor/maintainer review because the remaining blocker is provider API semantics plus missing real behavior proof, not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: Voice/Talk parity: TTS provider fallback + tts.status
target.body:
## PR Lane 14 — Voice/Talk parity with Mac/main

Brings Windows TTS closer to the Mac/main Talk/voice experience at the **service/capability layer** (no chat composer changes). Two behavior gaps closed plus a stale-doc cleanup.

### What changed & why

**1. Graceful TTS provider fallback.** Windows TTS supports three providers — Piper (local, default), Windows (built-in, always available), ElevenLabs (cloud). Previously, if the requested/configured provider wasn't ready (no ElevenLabs key, or Piper voice not downloaded), `tts.speak` **threw** and the assistant went silent — even though the Windows built-in voice was available. Now `SpeakAsync` resolves an *effective* provider and falls back to Windows TTS instead of failing. The result reports `provider` (who actually spoke), `requestedProvider`, and `fellBack`.
- On fallback, the per-call `voiceId`/`model` are dropped so the Windows synth doesn't reject a piper/elevenlabs voice id.
- A stale/removed **configured** Windows voice now degrades to the system default voice rather than throwing, so the fallback always speaks. An **explicit per-call** Windows voice that's invalid still throws (caller error).

**2. New `tts.status` command** mirroring the existing `stt.status`. Reports `configuredProvider`, `effectiveProvider` (after fallback), `willFallBack`, and per-provider readiness (`ready | needs-api-key | needs-voice | voice-not-downloaded | unavailable`). PII-free — never echoes voice ids, key fragments, or device names. This gives a future Mac-style Talk control the wire data it needs to show provider status and "fell back to Windows voice".

**3. Stale docstring fix.** `PiperVoiceManager` carried a `TODO (pre-GA): SHA-256 verification` comment, but verification is already implemented for Whisper/Piper/Silero and enforced by `AssetHashPinningTests`. Removed the misleading TODO.

### Files touched
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `Shared/Capabilities/TtsCapability.cs` | `StatusCommand`, `ResolveEffectiveProvider` pure helper, readiness constants, `TtsProviderResolution`/`TtsProviderStatus`/`TtsStatusResult`, `RequestedProvider`/`FellBack` on result, status handler |
| `Tray.WinUI/.../TextToSpeech/TextToSpeechService.cs` | Fallback in `SpeakAsync`, `GetProviderReadiness`, `BuildReadyProviderSet`, `GetStatus`, stale-configured-voice handling |
| `Tray.WinUI/Services/NodeService.cs` | `OnTtsStatusAsync` wiring |
| `Shared/Models.cs` | `tts.status` in `DangerousCommands` (gated by `NodeTtsEnabled`) |
| `Shared/Mcp/McpToolBridge.cs`, `WinNode.Cli/skill.md` | Command docs (drift-test enforced) |
| `SetupEngine/SetupContext.cs` | Export `tts.status` in `gateway.nodes.allowCommands` |
| `Shared/Audio/PiperVoiceManager.cs` | Docstring fix |
| tests | `CapabilityTests`, `ModelsTests`, `SetupConfigTests` |

### Behavior summary
- `tts.speak` no longer hard-fails on an unconfigured provider — it speaks (Windows) and reports `fellBack:true`.
- `tts.status` is a new read-only, PII-free readiness probe, gated behind `NodeTtsEnabled` (off by default).
- No UI/composer changes; Voice Settings page untouched.

### Validation
- `./build.ps1` — all 5 projects ✅
- `Shared.Tests` 2077 ✅ · `Tray.Tests` 961 ✅ · `WinNode.Cli.Tests` 120 ✅ (incl. skill.md↔registry drift) · `SetupEngine.Tests` 237 ✅
- Two rubber-duck reviews (gpt-5.5 + claude-opus-4.8). gpt-5.5 caught the missing setup-allowlist export (fixed) and a stale-configured-Windows-voice fallback gap (fixed). No outstanding blockers.

### Risks / limitations / deferred
- **Silent degradation on an explicit per-call provider**: an explicit `provider:"piper"` with an undownloaded voice now yields a Windows voice rather than the old actionable error. Intentional (goal = don't fail); info is preserved in `fellBack`/`requestedProvider`/`tts.status`. The chat-controls workstream should surface these to the user.
- **`willFallBack` is a configured-defaults snapshot** — a `tts.speak` with its own `voiceId` may avoid a fallback the snapshot predicted. Documented.
- **Deferred (chat-controls workstream)**: Mac's realtime in-chat Talk start/stop control + inline provider status text were intentionally not built here to avoid composer conflicts. The wire data is now ready for it.
- **Classification**: `tts.status` is Dangerous (consistent with `stt.status`); a Safe classification (like `device.status`) would also be defensible since it's read-only/PII-free.

Low blast radius: additive command + a fallback that only triggers on the previously-throwing path; no schema/storage/connection changes.
target.author: bkudiess
